name: Lee Barone
headline: Frontend developer for teams that care about UX, accessibility and measurable outcomes.
shortBio: I started in visual communication and grew into implementation-heavy web work across ecommerce, accessibility, analytics and brand systems.
I am strongest in frontend roles where implementation quality, user experience and cross-functional collaboration all matter, not just raw ticket throughput.
siteDescription: Career workspace, resume site and project library for Lee Barone.

liveSite.audience: Recruiters, hiring managers and team leads assessing fit for frontend roles in product, e-commerce and digital teams.
liveSite.availability: Open to frontend roles with meaningful product ownership, strong collaboration and room to keep growing technically over the long term.

proofStats.label: Web delivery from visual design through to frontend implementation
Shopify stores maintained across a live multi-brand ecommerce portfolio
Accessibility triage, remediation and implementation coordination
Analytics, data layer and tracking implementation across multiple platforms

idealEnvironment: Clear ownership with room for mentorship and growth
Teams that value accessibility, user experience and measurable improvement
Collaborative environments across product, design, marketing and engineering

valuePillars.title: Frontend execution with context
valuePillars.copy: I do not approach frontend as isolated tickets. I work from user journey, brand clarity and business outcome back into the implementation.

valuePillars.title: UX, accessibility and measurement
valuePillars.copy: My strongest work usually improves clarity, accessibility and tracking at the same time, so teams get a better experience and a better signal on what is working.

valuePillars.title: Design-aware collaboration
valuePillars.copy: Because I come from visual communication and digital design, I translate well between design, marketing and implementation teams without losing the details.
